channel.title: EO Glossary
channel.description: Community thesaurus of terms and definitions for Earth Observation sciences, maintained by CEOS and the EC Knowledge Centre for Earth Observation (KCEO).
channel.dc:creator: CEOS & EC KCEO

item.title: Version Control
item.description: Method of working with collections, similar to the version control systems used in traditional software development but optimized to allow better processing of data and collaboration in the context...

item.title: Vertical Levels
item.description: Levels of a vertical discretization.

item.title: Uncertainty
item.description: Parameter, associated with the result of a measurement, that characterises the dispersion of the values that could reasonably be attributed to the measurand.

item.title: Update Frequency
item.description: Frequency at which the product is available to users.

item.title: User
item.description: Entity (person, organization, institution, etc.) requesting data or information with specified characteristics.

item.title: User Interface
item.description: Set of all the components of an interactive system that provide information and controls for the User to accomplish specific tasks with the interactive system.

item.title: Validation
item.description: Process of assessing, by independent means, the quality of the data products derived from the system outputs.

item.title: Value
item.description: Element of a type domain.

item.title: Verification
item.description: Provision of objective evidence that a given item fulfils specified requirements.

item.title: Thematic Resolution
item.description: Level of categorical detail of information expressed by the number of classes.

item.title: Thematic Uncertainty
item.description: Uncertainty associated with the method of classification.

item.title: Time of Day
item.description: Time of the day at which the variable or parameter is observed or simulated by a model.

item.title: Time of Year
item.description: Time of the year at which the variable is observed or simulated by a model.

item.title: Timeliness
item.description: Period between the moment of requesting information and the moment of availability of information.

item.title: Tolerance
item.description: ### Notes.

item.title: Traceability
item.description: Property of a measurement result whereby the result can be related to a Reference through a documented unbroken chain of calibrations, each contributing to the measurement uncertainty.

item.title: Trait
item.description: Characteristic belonging to an entity and referenced by an identifier.

item.title: Systematic Error
item.description: Component of measurement error that remains constant or varies predictably across replicate measurements.

item.title: Telemetry
item.description: Automated transmission of payload data (science, engineering) from a platform to receiving equipment via telemetry link, excluding commands, tracking, memory transfers, audio, or video.

item.title: Temporal Consistency
item.description: Condition where the temporal statistical properties of the Sample depend only on the underlying physical processes and not on other factors such as fusing different products or sensors.

item.title: Temporal Extent
item.description: Period during which data was collected, observations were made, or for which the model was run.

item.title: Temporal Reporting Period
item.description: Time Period used for reporting and for which the information should be aggregated from the native Temporal Resolution.

item.title: Temporal Resolution
item.description: Regular interval between observations or model time steps that determines the smallest resolvable event or process.

item.title: Temporal Revisit
item.description: Interval between successive observations.

item.title: Spatial Extent
item.description: Zone or region of space described by a geographic identifier in the form of a label or code, or by a bounding box.

item.title: Spatial Reporting Unit
item.description: Smallest spatial object of interest that may be used for reporting and for which the information should be aggregated.

item.title: Spatial Resolution
item.description: ### Notes.

item.title: Spectral Band
item.description: Part of the electromagnetic spectrum of specific wavelengths.

item.title: Spectral Bandwidth
item.description: Range of the Spectral Band.

item.title: Spectral Resolution
item.description: Measure of the ability to resolve features in the electromagnetic spectrum.

item.title: Stability
item.description: Maximum acceptable change in Uncertainty per decade.

item.title: Standard Uncertainty
item.description: Standard deviation of the probability distribution describing the spread of possible values in quantitative/continuous data.

item.title: Steward
item.description: Entity responsible for the safe custody, transport, storage of the data and implementation of business rules.

item.title: Search and Discovery
item.description: Process of locating information using search criteria and obtaining details on available products via searchable metadata, browse images, and catalogue services.

item.title: Sensor
item.description: Device or instrument suited (through physical/chemical interaction) to measure one or more properties of a Phenomenon and thus collect factual/objective data.

item.title: Service
item.description: Output of an organization involving at least one activity performed between the organization and the customer, or a software component in a service-oriented architecture providing functionality or ...

item.title: Source Data
item.description: Data required for producing an Earth observation collection, including unenhanced satellite mission data, precision-corrected Level-1 radiance, or other pre-processed formats meeting program requir...

item.title: Spatial Completeness
item.description: Presence or absence of gaps, which are missing values in an otherwise continuous spatial data series.

item.title: Spatial Consistency
item.description: Condition where the spatial statistical properties of the data depend only on the underlying physical processes and not on other factors such as fusing different products or sensors.

item.title: Representativeness
item.description: Quality or characteristic of a measurement or Observation to accurately represent a specific geographic location, time Period, or environmental condition.

item.title: Reproducibility
item.description: Validity of study results through exact reproduction using the same data and methods to verify authenticity.

item.title: Resolution
item.description: ### Notes.

item.title: Sample
item.description: Subset of one or more entities.

item.title: Satellite Data
item.description: Data comprising housekeeping telemetry and payload data transmitted via the payload telemetry link.

item.title: Quality Indicator
item.description: Indicator providing sufficient information to allow all users to readily evaluate the fitness for purpose of the data or derived product.

item.title: Quantity
item.description: Property having a magnitude that can be expressed as a number from a continuous and contiguous range and a reference.

item.title: Random Error
item.description: Component of measurement error that varies unpredictably in replicate measurements.

item.title: Reference
item.description: Phenomenon whose values for specific properties are known and understood with an Uncertainty significantly lower (quantify?) than that of the Observation with which it is compared.

item.title: Remote Sensing
item.description: Type of Observation performed at a significant distance from a Phenomenon.

item.title: Repeatability
item.description: Measurement precision under conditions including the same procedure, location, equipment, and replicate measurements on similar objects over time.

item.title: Replicability
item.description: Property enabling other researchers to conduct an independent study with different data and similar but not identical methods, yet arriving at results that confirm the original study's hypothesis.

item.title: Product
item.description: Electronic data package distributable to users; content is derived from instrument data via processing involving ancillary and auxiliary data.

item.title: Property
item.description: Observable Trait.

item.title: Provisional Product
item.description: Products in a research and development phase not yet published into a standardized collection.

item.title: Quality Control
item.description: Activities to check and optimize data accuracy and precision after collection; part of quality management focused on fulfilling quality requirements.

item.title: Policy Objective
item.description: Desired outcome that policymakers wish to achieve.

item.title: Policy Target
item.description: Specific level or rate set for the Policy Objective.

item.title: Position
item.description: Place referenced by a single set of coordinates within a coordinate reference system.

item.title: Preservation
item.description: Processes and operations used to ensure data technical and intellectual survival through time.

item.title: Process
item.description: Series of activities that interact to produce a result.

item.title: Period
item.description: Particular era or span of time.

item.title: Period Identifier
item.description: Temporal reference in the form of a label or code that identifies a Period.

item.title: Phenomenon
item.description: Entity with at least one Property referenced by an identifier.

item.title: Place
item.description: Part of any space referenced by an identifier.

item.title: Policy
item.description: Deliberate system of guidelines to guide decisions and achieve rational outcomes.

item.title: Policy Cycle
item.description: Well-established concept, typically taught as the rational model of policy decision-making.

item.title: Policy File
item.description: Policy documents that represent the official written reference for a given Policy.

item.title: Policy Making
item.description: Process by which governments translate their political vision into programmes and actions to deliver outcomes — desired change in the real world.

item.title: Policy Milestone
item.description: Markers of significant progress in addressing specific issues, or crucial decision points where significant choices are made.

item.title: Outcome
item.description: ### Notes.

item.title: Path
item.description: Line defined in an algorithmic way using at least two positions (can be open-ended).

item.title: Minimum Mapping Unit
item.description: The area of the smallest Feature that is still represented on a map.

item.title: Minimum Mapping Width
item.description: The width of the smallest linear Feature that is still represented on a map.

item.title: Model
item.description: An, often numeric, theoretical (or otherwise abstract) representation of an Entity intended to generate Data describing one or more of its traits (as a result of pre-set conditions).

item.title: Near Real Time Data
item.description: Data available for use with a specified (small and application dependent) latency, typically 3 hours for meteorological applications.

item.title: Object
item.description: Entity with a well-defined boundary and identity that encapsulates state and behaviour.

item.title: Observation
item.description: Act of determining the Value of a Property by interacting in a reproducible way with the Phenomenon using a Sensor.

item.title: Obsolete Product or Collection
item.description: Products or collections which have been superseded by later versions of a product or collection.

item.title: Long Term Preservation
item.description: The act of maintaining information in a correct and independently understandable form over the long term.

item.title: Measurand
item.description: Particular Quantity subject to Measurement.

item.title: Measurement
item.description: Observation of a Quantity.

item.title: Measurement Uncertainty
item.description: Uncertainty associated with the method of Measurement.

item.title: Metadata
item.description: Data describing other data, providing an understanding of the content and utility of a product or collection.

item.title: Knowledge Transfer File
item.description: ### Notes

item.title: Laboratory Observation
item.description: Usually in-situ observations in which the Object or Phenomenon is isolated from other systems or altered in its original conditions or environment.

item.title: Latency
item.description: Period between the end of sensing of a Phenomenon to the beginning of availability of a specific product.

item.title: Lead Time
item.description: Minimum time of interval between an Observation being tasked and being performed.

item.title: Lithosphere
item.description: The solid, inanimate outer layer of the Geosphere.

item.title: Location
item.description: Particular Place referenced by an identifier.

item.title: Location Error
item.description: Measure of the agreement between the represented Location of an Object and the true Location.

item.title: Instance
item.description: One operational incarnation of an element with its configuration.

item.title: Instrument
item.description: Hardware system that collects scientific or operational data, or a hardware-integrated collection of one or more sensors contributing data of one type to an investigation.

item.title: Instrument Data
item.description: Data specifically associated with the instrument, either because it was generated by the instrument or included in data packets identified with that instrument.

item.title: Granule
item.description: The smallest aggregation of data which is independently managed (i.e.

item.title: Grid
item.description: Network composed of two or more sets of curves in which the members of each set intersect the members of the other sets in an algorithmic way.

item.title: Hydrosphere
item.description: The masses of liquid water, such as oceans, lakes, and rivers.

item.title: Impact
item.description: Regarded long-term according to X.

item.title: In-Situ Observation
item.description: Observations performed in the same Place where a Phenomenon occurs, normally without isolating it from other systems (its environment) or altering its pre-Observation state.

item.title: Geographic Coordinate Reference System
item.description: Coordinate Reference System (CRS) that has a geodetic Reference frame and an ellipsoidal coordinate system.

item.title: Geographic Data
item.description: Data with implicit or explicit reference to a Location relative to the Earth.

item.title: Geographic Grid
item.description: Regular grid based on a Geographic Coordinate Reference System (CRS).

item.title: Geographic Identifier
item.description: Spatial reference in the form of a label or code that identifies a Location.

item.title: Geolocating
item.description: Geopositioning of an Object using a Physical Sensor Model or a True Replacement Model.

item.title: Geolocation Information
item.description: Information used to determine geographic Location.

item.title: Geopositioning
item.description: Determination of the geographic Position of a point (0D) or linear (1D) Feature.

item.title: Georectifying
item.description: Correcting for positional displacement with respect to the surface of the Earth.

item.title: Geospatial Data
item.description: Data consisting of, derived from, or relating to information that is directly linked to specific geographical locations.

item.title: Geosphere
item.description: The masses of minerals that constitute planet Earth.

item.title: Final Product Version
item.description: Product within an EO Collection that is not expected to be updated.

item.title: Forecast Range
item.description: The forecast period.

item.title: Geocoding
item.description: Translation of one form of Location into another.

item.title: EO Space Data
item.description: Earth Observation data generated by spaceborne missions or instruments owned by public or private organizations.

item.title: Equivalence
item.description: ### Notes.

item.title: Error
item.description: Measured quantity value minus a reference quantity value.

item.title: Expanded Uncertainty
item.description: Standard Uncertainty multiplied by a coverage factor, k.

item.title: Feature
item.description: Abstraction of real-world phenomena.

item.title: Duration
item.description: Non-negative interval Quantity of time equal to the difference between the final and initial instants of a time interval.

item.title: Dwell Time
item.description: Time that an antenna beam spends on a target.

item.title: Earth Observation
item.description: Science domain dealing with technologies and methods of collecting and analyzing observations about the different Earth spheres.

item.title: Earth Observation Based Output
item.description: Higher-level processing and integration output (indicative level 4 and beyond) that goes beyond basic data processing.

item.title: Earth Observation Product
item.description: Direct output from standardized satellite data processing chains (indicative levels 2 and 3), representing fundamental geophysical and biophysical variables.

item.title: Earth Spheres
item.description: Spheres dividing planet Earth into subsystems, which are collectives of (physical) matter sharing specific traits.

item.title: Entity
item.description: Something that has separate and distinct existence and objective or conceptual reality.

item.title: Environmental Process
item.description: ### Notes.

item.title: Decommissioned Product
item.description: Products and collections that have been superseded by new versions and are no longer available.

item.title: Deprecated Product
item.description: Products and collections that have been superseded by a newer version and are still available, but whose use is discouraged since they will be decommissioned in the future.

item.title: Derivative Product
item.description: Product additionally processed from baseline or unenhanced mission data.

item.title: Dispose
item.description: Action of permanently and irrecoverably removing all copies of data held by an organization.

item.title: Data Producer
item.description: Entity or organization that develops novel data products or improved algorithms, including system developers, operators, researchers, and data stewards responsible for data production or collection.

item.title: Data Format
item.description: Structured Data that is machine readable and can be automatically read and processed by a computer, such as HDF, NetCDF, CSV, JSON, XML, etc.

item.title: Data Licencing
item.description: Legal instrument by which a copyright holder may grant rights over the protected work.

item.title: Data Manager
item.description: A role responsible for the management and organization of data assets.

item.title: Custodian
item.description: Entity responsible for the safe custody, transport, storage of the data and implementation of business rules.

item.title: Data
item.description: Value and possibly Uncertainty of a Trait of a specific Entity.

item.title: Data Access
item.description: Service to disseminate Data.

item.title: Consistency
item.description: ### Notes.

item.title: Continuity
item.description: ### Notes.

item.title: Copernicus Service Provider
item.description: Institutional body, organisation or programme that provides reliable, trusted (authoritative?) EO Information and that has the financial resources to sustain the provision.

item.title: Covariance Matrix
item.description: Symmetric positive semi-definite matrix of dimension N × N associated with an estimate of a real vector quantity of dimension N × 1, containing on its diagonal the squares of the standard uncertain...

item.title: Coverage Interval
item.description: Range or specific subset of values associated with a coverage dataset.

item.title: Cryosphere
item.description: Masses of frozen water, such as sea ice, ice shields, glaciers, and snow.

item.title: Climate Projection
item.description: Simulated response of the climate system to a scenario of future emission or concentration of greenhouse gases (GHGs) and aerosols, generally derived using climate models.

item.title: Collection
item.description: Group of products that have been processed using a consistent radiometric calibration, geometric registration base, data/metadata format, and version of processing algorithms.

item.title: Collection Update
item.description: Minor revisions to a relatively small number of products within a collection for the purpose of correcting errors and maintaining consistent quality.

item.title: Collection Upgrade
item.description: New and improved major version of all products within a collection due to comprehensive reprocessing.

item.title: Confidence Interval
item.description: Probability that the Quantity lies in the interval, conditioned on the measuring or modelling assumptions.

item.title: Business Continuity
item.description: An organisation's readiness to continue functioning during times of disruption.

item.title: Cartographic Projection
item.description: ### Notes.

item.title: Cell
item.description: Spatial, spatio-temporal zone or temporal unit of geometry with dimensionality greater than 0, associated with a zone.

item.title: Characteristic
item.description: Distinguishing Feature.

item.title: Classification System
item.description: Nomenclature used to classify Information into categorical thematic classes (e.g., EUNIS, MAES ecosystems, Corine Land Cover, LCCS).

item.title: Classification System Levels
item.description: Levels in a hierarchical Classification System.

item.title: Band Central Wavelength
item.description: Single wavelength Value within the sensitivity interval of a spectroradiometric Sensor which represents the respective band.

item.title: Baseline
item.description: Reference Period, time or measure against which the Information is assessed or compared.

item.title: Biosphere
item.description: Masses of living organisms, such as vegetation and animals, including dead but still connected parts such as roots, trunks or branches.

item.title: Area of Interest
item.description: Zone in 2D or 3D for which the Information or Data is requested.

item.title: Area of Representativeness
item.description: Area (or volume) where concentration does not deviate from the station value by more than a specified range.

item.title: Atmosphere
item.description: Layer of gases, commonly known as air, including suspended liquid and solid particles known as aerosols (incl.

item.title: Backup
item.description: General practice of making copies of current baseline and derived products of EO Collections at specified time intervals, and storing those copies in a manner that will not affect the data if the p...

item.title: Anthroposphere
item.description: One of the Earth spheres consisting predominantly of matter processed by humans, such as construction wood, bricks, concrete, asphalt, glass, or plastics.

item.title: Algorithm
item.description: Series of steps or rules for solving a problem or performing a task, especially by a computer.

item.title: Tagging
item.description: Process of adding tags or annotations to components of unstructured data at a granular level to prepare it for analysis, enabling deeper insight beyond broad categorization.

item.title: Subscription
item.description: Service providing regular access to a predefined set of products, typically delivered online, with user enrollment via subscription requests and no further ordering required.

item.title: Subscription Service
item.description: Service that notifies subscribers of data updates.

item.title: Subsetting
item.description: Extraction of a multi-dimensional rectangular pixel array from a data granule, defined by starting location and count along each dimension, or delivery of subset data based on time, space, variable...

item.title: Swath
item.description: Area covered by spatial samples collected during a scan of a spaceborne instrument, determined by its incidence angle.

item.title: Stewardship
item.description: Responsibility for planning, management, and certification of digital Earth observation data sets throughout mission phases, including curation, preservation, governance, and resource allocation fo...

item.title: Structured Data
item.description: Data with explicit structural relations between elements encoded in its storage format, such as XML or JSON, enabling logical interpretation distinct from visual layout.

item.title: Structured Error
item.description: Errors in satellite imagery affecting multiple pixels non-uniformly, where errors are correlated across affected regions due to localized systematic or random effects.

item.title: User Engagement
item.description: Evidence-based practice of understanding user needs, challenges, and work environments to deliver useful, usable, and used products and services through ongoing mutual exchange.

item.title: User Guide
item.description: Documentation intended to assist users in using a particular product, service, or application.

item.title: Uniform Resource Identifier
item.description: Compact sequence of characters identifying an abstract or physical resource on the Internet.

item.title: Uniform Resource Locator
item.description: Reference to a web resource specifying its location and retrieval mechanism.

item.title: Unstructured Data
item.description: Information in diverse forms that does not conform to conventional data models, complicating storage and management in relational databases.

item.title: Workflow
item.description: Orchestrated and repeatable pattern of business activity enabled by systematic organization of resources into processes.

item.title: Visualization
item.description: Display of an image representing a segment or subset of a data granule.

item.title: Variable
item.description: Physical property whose value determines the characteristics or behavior of a system, such as temperature, pressure, or density.

item.title: Standard Product
item.description: Agency-certified key products systematically acquired and generated through extensive spatial and temporal processing.

item.title: Software as a Service
item.description: Software hosted and maintained by a third party, accessed via the web, with data storage and monetization managed by the host.

item.title: Service Level Agreement
item.description: Agreement defining expected service levels, measurement metrics, and remedies or penalties for unmet thresholds, applicable between companies or internal departments.

item.title: Sensor Performance Monitoring
item.description: Long-term recording and trend analysis of parameters describing sensor quality and their level-0 outputs.

item.title: Secondary Data
item.description: Data used for processing or interpreting primary data.

item.title: Segregation
item.description: Earth observation products with degraded quality relative to nominal standards, such as those acquired during maneuvers or special campaigns, still useful but requiring clear indication of status a...

item.title: Search and Discovery Interface
item.description: Web application interface enabling users to search collection or granule metadata using terms such as location, time, and characteristics.

item.title: Science Stewards
item.description: Experts providing scientific oversight to ensure accurate data and metadata representation, guiding data quality, characterization, and usability requirements.

item.title: Scientific Data Formats
item.description: Structured, self-describing, open standards enabling N-dimensional scientific data representation via software libraries and machine-independent formats.

item.title: Retrieval
item.description: Physical transfer of data from a repository to the user.

item.title: Scene
item.description: Subset of instrument acquisition data segmented by time across the swath.

item.title: Science Data Segment Interface
item.description: System for assessing and verifying quality of Raw Data Records (RDRs), Sensor Data Records (SDRs), Environmental Data Records (EDRs), and Temperature Data Records (TDRs) through distributed Product...

item.title: Science Data System
item.description: System overseeing the lifecycle of science data from acquisition to distribution to maximize scientific return for researchers, decision makers, and society.

item.title: Restituted Orbit
item.description: Orbit determination using delayed telemetry data with a one-day latency, computed from a three-day moving window and typically sampled every 60 seconds with lower precision than precise orbits.

item.title: Resample
item.description: Technique of transforming a digital image to alter resolution, orientation, or sampling points for applications in medical, industrial, and remote sensing contexts.

item.title: Reprocessing
item.description: Systematic generation of a new product revision using archived lower-level products, typically triggered by an improved processing algorithm.

item.title: Representation Information
item.description: Information enabling digital data to be interpreted and used, including structural, semantic, and format descriptions, provided formally (e.g., XFDU) or informally (e.g., text).

item.title: Reformatting
item.description: Transformation process converting data from one format to another, including reorganization, relocation, or dropping of fields, using well-defined rules to preserve information integrity.

item.title: Reference Model
item.description: Conceptual framework of unifying concepts for understanding relationships among entities in an environment and developing consistent standards, particularly for archival systems preserving digital ...

item.title: Reconciliation
item.description: Process of matching processed output data with relevant input data to detect unexplained losses and establish the output as the master dataset for archiving and further use.

item.title: Readme
item.description: File providing metadata to ensure correct interpretation of associated data by users now or in the future.

item.title: Quarantine
item.description: Withholding of an Earth observation product from distribution due to severe technical or quality issues.

item.title: Radiometric Calibration
item.description: Determination of radiometric instrument performance across spatial, spectral, and temporal domains by relating output to true radiometric values.

item.title: Provenance Information
item.description: Information documenting the origin, history, modifications, inputs, algorithms used, and custody of content.

item.title: Proximity Computing
item.description: Computing data physically close to its source to minimize remote processing and data transfer.

item.title: Quality Assessment
item.description: Derivation of quality indicators sufficient to assess fulfillment of quality requirements.

item.title: Provenance
item.description: Information documenting the origin, history, modifications, inputs, algorithms used, and custody of content.

item.title: Production Request
item.description: Request for producing one or more products, typically tied to a user order and parameterized with processing, input, and optional output parameters.

item.title: Product Type Collection
item.description: Ensemble of all available products of a specific product type.

item.title: Product Team
item.description: Scientifically knowledgeable team ensuring products are generated, high quality, well documented, and delivered to the archive.

item.title: Product Quality Control
item.description: Determination of quality parameters for individual products through automated or manual activities such as quick look visualization.

item.title: Product Area
item.description: Suite of products organized by thematic category into foundational data, geophysical products, and analytical reports.

item.title: Processing
item.description: Generation of higher-level products from lower-level and auxiliary products using version-controlled core algorithms supplemented by administrative functions.

item.title: Producer
item.description: Role of persons or systems providing information to be preserved, including other OAISs or internal entities.

item.title: Process Validation
item.description: Established documented evidence providing high assurance that a process consistently produces products meeting predefined specifications and quality characteristics.

item.title: Precision
item.description: Closeness of agreement between indications or measured values obtained by replicate measurements on the same or similar objects under specified conditions.

item.title: Precise Orbit
item.description: Orbit model derived from all available satellite tracking data and dynamical corrections, achieving highest accuracy.

item.title: Platform
item.description: Support carrying instrument(s) or sensor(s), such as a spacecraft, aircraft, or ground-based platform.

item.title: Order Fulfillment Access Model
item.description: Group of data access services enabling delayed delivery due to non-immediate availability or need for formatting, transformation, or packaging.

item.title: Payload Data
item.description: Data transmitted via the payload telemetry link, presented as instrument data, platform ancillary data, and housekeeping telemetry after packet extraction and reordering.

item.title: Order Agreement
item.description: Agreement specifying physical delivery details of data, such as media type and format.

item.title: Orbit Prediction and Determination
item.description: Generation of attitude and state vectors to describe spacecraft position over time, computed either in advance (predicted) or after observation (restituted or precise).

item.title: Operations
item.description: Activities directly related to the acquisition, archiving, distribution, and processing of mission-related information.

item.title: Orbit
item.description: Path in space described by a satellite revolving around the Earth under mutual gravitational attraction.

item.title: Monitoring and Control
item.description: Function ensuring nominal operation of ground segment resources and making activities visible and traceable through operator interaction for failure handling.

item.title: Master Data Record
item.description: Consolidated and quality-checked data record resulting from consolidation procedures, used for preservation, dissemination, and further processing in Earth Observation data stewardship.

item.title: Measurement Procedure
item.description: Detailed description of a measurement based on one or more principles and methods, including the measurement model and all calculations to obtain a result.

item.title: Migration
item.description: Transfer of digital information to new hardware or software environments to preserve it and overcome technological obsolescence, excluding format conversion as reprocessing.

item.title: Mission Planning
item.description: Computation of non-conflicting timelines for space payload activities and ground station reception, accounting for budgets, capacities, and station availabilities, including cross-mission conflict ...

item.title: Logical Record
item.description: Logical unit of information defined by content, function, and use rather than physical storage; portions may span multiple physical records or multiple logical records may reside in one.

item.title: Long Term Archive
item.description: System for long-term storage of data, separated from primary media for compliance or archival purposes, organized as files or cataloged structures.

item.title: Maintenance
item.description: Activities sustaining equipment and software in current operational configuration.

item.title: Open Source
item.description: Software with source code available under an open license, permitting free use, inspection, modification, and redistribution by users with technical capability.

item.title: Operational Data Store
item.description: Database optimized for frequent queries on transactional data, updated during business operations and typically serving as an interim staging area for a data warehouse.

item.title: Open Science
item.description: Practice of science adhering to open principles, including open access publishing, sharing research data and source code as open data, and using open-source tools.

item.title: Open Data
item.description: Data freely accessible, usable, modifiable, and shareable for any purpose, subject only to attribution or share-alike requirements, as defined by the Open Definition.

item.title: Open Archival Information System
item.description: Organization with people and systems responsible for preserving information and making it available to a designated community, meeting defined archival responsibilities.

item.title: Online Access
item.description: Function providing direct online availability of products for user download.

item.title: Open Access
item.description: Principle that published research outputs, especially from publicly-funded work, should be freely accessible to all without subscription barriers.

item.title: On-the-fly Processing
item.description: Fully automated generation of a requested product for immediate download, with processing time not exceeding 10 minutes.

item.title: Near Real Time
item.description: Data available with a small, application-dependent latency, typically 3 hours in meteorological applications.

item.title: Notification
item.description: Function that informs registered recipients—internal ground segment elements or external users—about events in payload data.

item.title: Knowledge Information Package
item.description: Information package containing consolidated data records and associated knowledge for a specific product type of an EO mission instrument, tailored to the designated community.

item.title: Inventory Service
item.description: Part of a Catalogue Service containing information to identify and retrieve individual granules given variable ranges, including extracted granule metadata and ordering details.

item.title: Inventory
item.description: Organization capability for archiving management, enabling grouping, searching, and identification of data products for retrieval, statistics, and reorganization; also refers to the list of availab...

item.title: Interoperable Products
item.description: Set of two or more ARD products sufficiently documented to enable processing across a continuum of geometric and/or radiometric standards for direct quantitative comparison.

item.title: Interoperability
item.description: Ability of different information technology systems and software applications to communicate, exchange data, and use the exchanged information, including interoperable discovery and access across c...

item.title: Interferometric Baseline
item.description: Accurate determination of orbital distances between satellites with compatible orbits, required for SAR and differential interferometry applications.

item.title: Instrument Calibration
item.description: Determination of parameters describing instrument characteristics for generating calibrated and comparable physical values, varying by instrument, mode, and over time due to degradation.

item.title: Information Package
item.description: Logical container containing optional content information and associated preservation description information, with packaging and package description information for identification and search.

item.title: Information Product
item.description: Product formed by selecting, acquiring, evaluating, analyzing, synthesizing, assessing, restructuring, aggregating, translating, packaging, or repackaging data and information from various sources.

item.title: Information System
item.description: System composed of hardware, software, and operational procedures for processing, storing, or transmitting data.

item.title: Ingest
item.description: Process of accepting data from sources such as ground segment reception or processing, performing quality checks, extracting metadata including browse images, and forming a data product for archivi...

item.title: Ingestion
item.description: Process of accepting data from sources such as ground segment reception or processing, performing quality checks, extracting metadata including browse images, and forming a data product for archivi...

item.title: Influence quantity
item.description: Quantity that affects the relation between the indication and the measurement result without affecting the measured quantity.

item.title: Information
item.description: Result of organizing, interpreting, categorizing, or classifying data to assign meaning understandable by the addressee.

item.title: Information Object
item.description: Data object combined with its representation information, enabling interpretation and exchange of meaning.

item.title: Import
item.description: Automated or semi-automated input of data sets between different software applications.

item.title: Homogeneous Data
item.description: Data drawn from a single population with constant external influences throughout the observation period.

item.title: Fundamental Climate Data Record
item.description: Long-term, stabilised record of calibrated sensor observations in physical units with quantified uncertainty and associated ancillary data.

item.title: Fused Product
item.description: Derived data product created by merging two or more fully interoperable products into a single, non-reversible dataset.

item.title: Geometrical Resolution
item.description: Ability of a sensor system to distinguish signals from neighboring object structures.

item.title: Global Attribute
item.description: Metadata providing contextual information about a dataset as a whole to enable independent usability.

item.title: Fiducial Reference Measurements
item.description: Independent measurements traceable to SI standards, produced with documented uncertainties to validate satellite data.

item.title: Flight Operations Segment
item.description: Personnel and elements responsible for planning, executing, and evaluating control of the space segment in orbit.

item.title: Frame
item.description: Fixed-size product generated from a longer data segment, with fixed frames having predefined geographical footprints and floating frames starting at user-chosen positions.

item.title: Function
item.description: Self-contained software routine that performs a task by processing input values and optionally returning results.

item.title: F.A.I.R. principle
item.description: Guiding principles for enhancing Findability, Accessibility, Interoperability, and Reuse of digital assets with machine-actionability.

item.title: Facility
item.description: Grouping of functional activities operated together, including their hardware instantiation in an Operations Center.

item.title: Essential Climate Variable
item.description: Physical, chemical, or biological variable or group of linked variables critical to characterizing Earth’s climate and providing empirical evidence for climate understanding, prediction, and servic...

item.title: Exploitation
item.description: Services, applications, and platforms that process and utilize space mission data to derive meaningful information and generate benefit.

item.title: Exploitation Platform
item.description: Collaborative Big Data processing environment enabling communities to share algorithms, data, and expertise for mission-specific or multi-mission research and service provisioning.

item.title: Export
item.description: Saving a copy of a document, database, image, or video in a file format compatible with another application.

item.title: Environmental Data Record
item.description: Recorded and derived observations and measurements of physical, chemical, biological, geological, and geophysical properties of the Earth system, including correlative data, metadata, documentation...

item.title: EO Space Data Producers
item.description: Private or public institutes responsible for Earth Observation space missions and the production of derived data products at various levels.

item.title: EO Space Data Users
item.description: User communities that rely on Earth Observation space data and products for application areas such as climate monitoring, agriculture, or disaster response.

item.title: Domain Expert
item.description: Subject-matter expert with authoritative knowledge in a specific domain, especially in expert systems development.

item.title: Data Record
item.description: Data or information in a fixed form with consistent content, structure, and context; for Earth observation, it includes instrument data, browse data, ancillary and auxiliary data, calibration and v...

item.title: Data Science
item.description: Multidisciplinary approach to extracting actionable insights from large datasets through data preparation, advanced analysis, and result visualization to inform stakeholder decisions.

item.title: Data set
item.description: Logically meaningful collection of similar or related data with shared characteristics—such as source, processing level, or resolution—grouped to meet specific coverage or revisit requirements.

item.title: Data Migration
item.description: Transfer of data between storage types, formats, or systems with minimal change, often driven by acquisitions, relocation, or system scaling, and used to rethink data strategy and architecture.

item.title: Data Processing
item.description: Collective set of data actions including collection, retention, logging, generation, transformation, use, disclosure, sharing, transmission, and disposal.

item.title: Data Quality
item.description: Metric—such as a number, graph, uncertainty budget, or flag—that enables users to evaluate the fitness for purpose of data or derived products.

item.title: Data Management
item.description: Services and functions for populating, maintaining, and accessing diverse information such as catalogs, processing algorithms, access statistics, security controls, and OAIS policies.

item.title: Data Management Plan
item.description: Plan describing how scientific data will be generated, processed, distributed, analyzed, archived, managed, described, stored, protected, and standardized throughout a project's lifecycle.

item.title: Data Management Services
item.description: Services that enforce standards, ingest data, develop collections and products, maintain databases, ensure secure archival, provide user- and machine-accessible interfaces, assist users, migrate te...

item.title: Dissemination
item.description: Function that delivers final products to users via physical media, electronic distribution (e.g., ftp-push), or online server access (e.g., ftp-pull), including preparation of delivery media and ma...

item.title: Dissemination Request
item.description: Request for dissemination of specific products, parameterized by delivery method, medium, address, and product selection criteria, often part of a bulk dissemination request.

item.title: Data Warehouse
item.description: Unified database that holds and makes accessible an organization’s entire business information with subject-based classification, normalized design, and enterprise-wide data organization.

item.title: Dataset Landing Page
item.description: Web page containing descriptive information and relevant documentation to facilitate understanding and use of a dataset.

item.title: Derived Products
item.description: Higher-level products (level 1b through 4) generated by applying calibration, geo-location transformations, and algorithms to produce sensor units or gridded geophysical parameters.

item.title: Description Information
item.description: Set of information identifying an archived dataset for long-term preservation, including spatial mission and payload details, data composition, availability, time span, volume, storage media, and a...

item.title: Designated Community
item.description: Scientific or other user group in the same or related disciplines with a vested interest in the long-term availability and usability of a preserved dataset.

item.title: Data Stewards
item.description: Individuals responsible for establishing, maintaining, and being accountable for the quality, integrity, documentation, and preservation of environmental data.

item.title: Data Stewardship
item.description: Application of rigorous analyses and oversight to ensure data sets meet user needs, including metadata documentation, observing system feedback, inter-comparison, reprocessing, and recommendation o...

item.title: Data Transformation
item.description: Process of converting data or information from one format to another, typically from a source system to a required destination format, including language or structural transformations for platform ...

item.title: Coordinate Reference System
item.description: Coordinate-based framework for defining and locating geographical entities locally, regionally, or globally.

item.title: Curation
item.description: Value-adding, organizational, presentational, and preservation activities to establish, increase, and sustain the value of Earth observation data over its lifecycle.

item.title: Data Architect
item.description: Senior role responsible for translating business requirements into technology requirements and designing an organization’s enterprise data management framework, including standards, processes, and ...

item.title: Consumer
item.description: Role of persons or client systems that interact with OAIS services to find and access preserved information.

item.title: Content Information
item.description: Information object comprising content data and representation information, serving as the primary target for preservation.

item.title: Context Information
item.description: Information documenting the relationships of content information to its environment, including its creation purpose and connections to other content objects.

item.title: Controlled Unclassified Information
item.description: Information requiring safeguarding or dissemination controls under law, regulation, or government policy, excluding classified national security information or atomic energy information.

item.title: Cloud Computing
item.description: Model for on-demand network access to a shared pool of configurable IT resources (e.g., networks, servers, storage, applications, and services) that can be rapidly provisioned and released with min...

item.title: Data Integration
item.description: Process of making datasets from different sources compatible through consistent naming, units, and coordinates to enable combined use.

item.title: Data Licensing
item.description: Legal instrument granting rights to use protected data, where open data requires an explicitly applied license conforming to the Open Definition.

item.title: Data Compression
item.description: Reduction of file size via compression algorithms, rendering the file unreadable without decompression.

item.title: Data Cube
item.description: Multi-dimensional array of values representing data along axes such as space, time, or other measurable dimensions.

item.title: Data Curator
item.description: Role responsible for annotating, publishing, and managing data to ensure long-term value, availability, and reusability.

item.title: Data Distributor
item.description: Entity that provides access to data for end users, ensuring accessibility and reliability across disciplinary boundaries.

item.title: Data fusion
item.description: Framework for combining data from multiple sources to produce higher-quality information, with quality defined by application context.

item.title: Data Catalog
item.description: System for discovering Earth observation data products, organized into collections with access controls based on product and user type.

item.title: Data Catalog Service
item.description: Process of exposing product or collection-level catalogs via standardized protocols such as OGC CSW.

item.title: Data Center
item.description: Facility that stores, maintains, and provides access to datasets, including data selection, replication, documentation, and tailored product generation.

item.title: Authoritative Source
item.description: Entity with verified access to accurate information from an issuing source to validate identity evidence during proofing.

item.title: Authorization of Use
item.description: Official management decision to authorize use of an information system based on an authorization package and accepted risk to operations, assets, and reputation.

item.title: Auxiliary Data
item.description: Data enhancing processing and utilization of main sensor data, collected via different processes or platforms, and usable as independent datasets.

item.title: Associated Knowledge
item.description: All information and tools relevant to instrument data that ensure its usability, legibility, and understandability.

item.title: Attitude Data
item.description: Data representing spacecraft orientation, pointing, calibration, alignment, Euler angles, quaternions, rates, biases, and associated accuracy parameters.

item.title: Archivist
item.description: Professional trained in preserving original materials and facilitating access, with expertise across the records life cycle and strong research skills.

item.title: Archive Operations
item.description: Functional entity managing day-to-day operations of an archive system, including hardware, software, monitoring, error recovery, maintenance, and reporting.

item.title: Archive
item.description: System for long-term storage of data products with guaranteed preservation.

item.title: Climatology
item.description: Long-term average of a climate variable, typically over 20–30 years, computed for specified time ranges such as monthly or daily means.

item.title: Calibration
item.description: Process of quantitatively defining a system's response to known, controlled inputs using reference measurements.

item.title: Calibration Data
item.description: Data required to calibrate instrument science data, engineering data, and spacecraft data, including pre-flight and in-flight measurements, calibration coefficients, and ground truth.

item.title: Catalogue
item.description: System that provides discovery of information on which Earth observation products can be obtained.

item.title: Catalogue Service
item.description: Functionality to expose or publish product-level catalogues using protocols such as the Open Geospatial Consortium's Catalogue Service for Web (CSW).

item.title: Climate Indices
item.description: Diagnostic time series representing the state of a climate system, derived from specific atmospheric or oceanic variables.

item.title: Big Data
item.description: Data sets so large or complex that traditional data processing applications are inadequate.

item.title: Browse
item.description: Process of viewing browse data or images to quickly assess them before accessing full-resolution or broader spatio-temporal data.

item.title: Browse Data
item.description: Subsets of data sets, other than directory and metadata, that facilitate user selection of specific data by providing reduced-resolution or simplified representations.

item.title: Ancillary Data
item.description: Data acquired on the same platform as the main sensor but not from the sensor itself, used primarily for processing instrument data.

item.title: Application
item.description: Computer program designed for specific end-user tasks, distinct from system or utility software.

item.title: Appraisal
item.description: Procedure for assessing the value and suitability of Earth observation space data sets for long-term preservation.

item.title: Archival Information Package
item.description: Information package containing content and preservation description information preserved within an OAIS.

item.title: Analytics
item.description: Systematic computational analysis of data or statistics to discover, interpret, and communicate meaningful patterns for decision-making.

item.title: Algorithm Theoretical Basis Document
item.description: Document detailing the theoretical foundation of an algorithm, including physical theory, mathematical procedures, and assumptions, updated with algorithm changes.

item.title: Analysis
item.description: Verification method using analytical techniques to demonstrate theoretical compliance with requirements through review, modeling, simulation, calculations, or data comparisons.

item.title: Analysis Ready Data
item.description: Processed data product geometrically referenced and minimally corrected to enable direct use without additional user processing.

item.title: Acquisition
item.description: Process from on-board recording and downlink to ground reconstruction of instrument source packets.

item.title: Acquisition Planning
item.description: Computation of a non-conflicting timeline for space segment and station reception activities, accounting for budgets, capacities, station availability, and cross-mission conflict resolution.

item.title: Administration Functional Entity
item.description: OAIS functional entity that controls day-to-day operation of other OAIS functional entities.

item.title: Access Rights Information
item.description: Information specifying access restrictions on content, including legal terms, licensing, access control, and rights enforcement specifications as defined in submission agreements.

item.title: Access Software
item.description: OAIS functional entity that makes archival information holdings and related services visible to consumers.

item.title: Accuracy
item.description: Closeness of agreement between a measured quantity value and a true quantity value of a measurand.

item.title: Access
item.description: Services or systems enabling data search, discovery, retrieval, and dissemination; may refer to functionality, service providers, or entities providing access.

item.title: Access Functional Entity
item.description: OAIS functional entity that makes archival information holdings and related services visible to consumers.

item.title: Application Programming Interface
item.description: According to Wikipedia, \"In computer programming, an application programming interface (API) is a set of routines, protocols, and tools for building software and applications.

item.title: Data Use Policy Agreement
item.description: Executed agreement between a data provider and a data recipient that specifies the terms under which the data can be used.

item.title: Maturity Matrix
item.description: Maturity Matrix/Model is a measurement of the ability of an organization for continuous improvement in a particular discipline.

item.title: Raw Data
item.description: Time-ordered telemetry data from the instrument before any processing has been applied.

item.title: Spatial Reference
item.description: Method by which location or coverage is designated (e.g., latitude and longitude).

item.title: Data Provider
item.description: An entity that archives and distributes data.

item.title: Inter-Project Agreement
item.description: IPAs are agreements between ESDIS and projects not managed by ESDIS.

item.title: Interface Control Document
item.description: ICDs are used to record design agreements for the interfaces between participating organizations.

item.title: Dead Band
item.description: maximum interval through which a value of a quantity being measured can be changed in both directions without producing a detectable change in the corresponding indication.

item.title: Homogenisation
item.description: Unlike harmonisation, homogenisation is where all satellites are forced to look the same such that when looking at the same location at the same time they would (in theory) give the same signal.

item.title: Point-to-Area Representativeness
item.description: The probability that a point measurement lies within a specific range of area-average (volume-average) concentration value.

item.title: Instantaneous Field of View
item.description: A measure of the spatial resolution of a remotesensing imaging system.

item.title: Climate Data Record
item.description: A time series of measurements of sufficientlength, consistency, and continuity to determineclimate variability and change.

item.title: Version
item.description: Versioning refers to saving new copies of your files when you make changes so that you can go back and retrieve specific versions of your files later.

item.title: Vicarious Calibration
item.description: Post-launch calibration of sensors that make use of natural or artificial sites on the surface of the Earth.

item.title: Virtual Archive Information Package
item.description: A ‘Virtual’ AIP (vAIP) is an NCEI specific realization of the Open Archival Information System Reference Model (OAIS RM) concept of the Archival Information Package (AIP).

item.title: Volume
item.description: A unit of physical storage medium which contains data.

item.title: Web Object Storage
item.description: Object Storage accessible through HTTPS.

item.title: Uncertainty of Measurement Method
item.description: Uncertainty associated with the method of measurement, as there can be other methods, some of them as yet unknown or in some way impractical, that would give systematically different results of app...

item.title: User Category
item.description: Classification of users in order to provide PDGS services with different access rights and service levels.

item.title: User Management
item.description: The user management function maintains information about registered users and supports registration, authentication and information needed for authorization.

item.title: User Support
item.description: User support is a function inside the payload data ground segment to support external users to interact with the segment, to handle user registration, inquiries, complaints.

item.title: Value-added Product
item.description: The production of a product in a manner that changes the physical state or form of the product and enhances its value, as demonstrated through a business plan.

item.title: Technology Stewards
item.description: Individuals who are responsible for establishing, maintaining, and being accountable for the.

item.title: Telemetry Data
item.description: Data stream of measured values (instrument science, instrument engineering, and spacecraftor platform engineering data), not including command, tracking, computer memory transfer, audio or video si...

item.title: Thumbnail
item.description: Small, very low resolution browse image which may be made available for users as part of the catalogue service .

item.title: Traceability Chain
item.description: Sequence of measurement standards and calibrations that is used to relate a measurement result to a reference.

item.title: Transcription
item.description: Migration of data from one storage medium to another (from old to new technologies) in order to preserve the data and for efficiency purposes.

item.title: Transformation
item.description: A digital migration in which there is an alteration to the content information or preservation Description information of an archival information package.

item.title: Type A evaluation of uncertainty
item.description: Method of evaluation of a component of measurement uncertainty by a statistical analysis of quantity values obtained by measurements under repeatability conditions.

item.title: Type B evaluation of uncertainty
item.description: Method of evaluation of a component of measurement uncertainty by means other than a statistical analysis of quantity values obtained by measurement.

item.title: Submission Agreement
item.description: The agreement reached between an OAIS and theproducer that specifies a data model for the datasubmission session.

item.title: Submission Information Package
item.description: An Information Package that is delivered by the Producer to the OAIS for use in the construction or update of one or more AIPs and/or the associated Descriptive Information.

item.title: System
item.description: Set of interrelated or interacting elements.

item.title: Standardisation
item.description: Establish an agreement (not necessarily obligatory) on precise procedures and concepts.

item.title: Storage
item.description: The permanent holding place for digital data, until purposely erased.

item.title: Self-Service Access Model
item.description: Self-Service Access Model - a group of data access services which a user can employ to obtain data of interest without any delay as the data are immediately available.

item.title: Semantic Information
item.description: The Representation Information that further describes the meaning beyond that provided by the Structure Information.

item.title: Sensitivity of a Measuring System
item.description: Quotient of the change in an indication of a measuring system and the corresponding change in a value of a quantity being measured.

item.title: Research Product
item.description: A data product produced at a science computing facility by a research algorithm for later migration to a community consensus algorithm and can be archived and distributed by an archive center.

item.title: Retention Period
item.description: The minimum amount of time that a key or other cryptographically related information should be retained.

item.title: Schema
item.description: The definition of a data structure such as a database or XML file.

item.title: Refreshment
item.description: A digital migration where the effect is to replacea media instance with a copy that is sufficiently exact so that all archival storage hardware and software continues to run as before.

item.title: Relative Standard Uncertainty
item.description: Standard measurement uncertainty divided by the absolute value of the measured quantity value.

item.title: Repackaging
item.description: Repackaging is a digital migration which alters the packaging information of the AIP.

item.title: Replication
item.description: A Digital Migration where there is no change to the Packaging Information, the Content Information, and the PDI.

item.title: Repository
item.description: A metadata repository, commonly referred to as a catalog, is accessed by client applications that support user queries.

item.title: Reprojection
item.description: Changing the coordinates values of a dataset from one coordinate system to another coordinate system.

item.title: Request
item.description: A request is the generic means to use a function of an element that provides the function as its service.

item.title: REST
item.description: A software architectural style that defines a common method for defining APIs for Web services.

item.title: Purge
item.description: To permanently and irrecoverably remove all copies of an Earth Observation data set held in an organization.

item.title: Quality
item.description: Degree to which a set of inherent characteristics of an object fulfils requirements.

item.title: Quality Assurance
item.description: Part of quality management focused on providing confidence that quality requirements will be fulfilled.

item.title: Quality Information
item.description: Secondary data required to assess the primary data set’s fitness for purpose, e.g.

item.title: Processing Baseline
item.description: A combination of processor versions, auxiliary data and other needed enablers that allows the generation of a coherent set of EO products.

item.title: Processing Levels
item.description: Raw Data

item.title: Predicted Orbit
item.description: These state vectors are calculated (e.g.

item.title: Preliminary Orbit
item.description: Preliminary orbits are based on the fast delivery tracking data.

item.title: Preservation Description Information
item.description: Reference, context, provenance, and fixity information, which is required for adequate preservation of the content information.

item.title: Preservation Workflow
item.description: A set of actions recommended for the preservation of an “EO Missions/Sensors data set” (i.e.

item.title: Preserved data set Content
item.description: The individual items making up a complete Earth Observation data se t to be preserved, including data records , and the a ssociated knowledge , i.e.

item.title: Primary Data
item.description: A set of results from a scientific observation, i.e.

item.title: Procedure
item.description: Specified way to carry out an activity or a process.

item.title: Outreach
item.description: The act of inviting users (or stakeholders more broadly) to engage in a mutually beneficial exchange.

item.title: Packaging Information
item.description: A description of the package, which allows the user to understand the structure of the information package.

item.title: Parameter
item.description: A measurable or derived variable occurring in the physical or digital world.

item.title: Payload Data Ground Segment
item.description: The personnel and elements performing mission operations related to payload data.

item.title: Physical Medium
item.description: Any physical material capable of holding data(e.g., pages, film, magnetic tape, optical disk, wire, silicon).

item.title: Positional Accuracy
item.description: Closeness of coordinate value to the true or accepted value in a specified reference system.

item.title: Operational Product
item.description: It is recognized as an important data source for the data repository and operational reporting.

item.title: Operational Scenario
item.description: Pre-defined end-to-end flow of operation characterized with respect to the implemented overall data flow and interaction between the external entities and the PDGS system components.

item.title: Operations Center
item.description: Physical location where “ Facilities ” are operated and/or coordinated, comprising infrastructure, human resources, operational procedures, etc.

item.title: Operator
item.description: Part of the PDGS being in charge of the management of the PDGS systems and operational procedures in order to provide the PDGS services to the users.

item.title: Monitoring
item.description: (1) systematic evaluation over time of some quantity

item.title: On-demand data set
item.description: Collection of products that are generated in response to a user’s request.

item.title: Logical Volume
item.description: That portion of a volume which is viewed by acomputer operating system as a volume.

item.title: Machine Learning
item.description: The use and development of computer systems that are able to learn and adapt without following explicit instructions, by using algorithms and statistical models to analyze and draw inferences from ...

item.title: Methodology
item.description: A generalised description of an approach or set of approaches to carry out a particular task.

item.title: Metrics
item.description: Tools designed to facilitate decision making and improve performance and accountability through collection, analysis, and reporting of relevant performance-related data.

item.title: Mission Phase
item.description: Mission Specific period characterized by a set of parameters (e.g.

item.title: Inventory System
item.description: A specific implementation of an inventory service.

item.title: Lifecycle
item.description: Evolution of a system, product, service, project, or other human-made entity from conception through retirement.

item.title: Lineage
item.description: The history of processing of a data element, which may include point-to-point data flows and the data actions performed upon the data element.

item.title: Instrument Source Packet
item.description: An individual packet of data formatted by the instrument and reconstituted from within the descrambled VCDUs.

item.title: Instrumental drift
item.description: Continuous or incremental change over time in indication, due to changes in metrological properties of a measuring instrument.

item.title: Intelligent Filtering
item.description: Software components within the management system that perform this preprocessing task.

item.title: Granule Metadata
item.description: These are metadata elements that describe a single granule of a data product.

item.title: Ground Sampling Distance
item.description: linear distance between pixel centres on the ground.

item.title: Ground Truth
item.description: Geophysical parameter data, measured or collected by other means than by the instrument itself, used as correlative or calibration/validation data for that instrument data.

item.title: Guide
item.description: The guide function is part of the user information function to provide information about satellites, sensors, product types and services.

item.title: Guide Service
item.description: Part of a Catalogue Service that provides detailed information concerning specific data sets which enable the user to make a detailed analysis of whether a data set or a specific granule within the...

item.title: Heterogeneous Data
item.description: Heterogeneous data are any data with high variability of data types and formats.

item.title: Housekeeping Telemetry
item.description: Housekeeping telemetry (HKTM) is all the telemetry necessary to monitor the health and status of the satellite and transmitted through the telemetry link.

item.title: In Situ Measurement
item.description: (1) a direct measurement of the measurand in its original place

item.title: Indication
item.description: Quantity value provided by a measuring instrument or a measuring system.

item.title: Fiducial
item.description: used as a fixed standard of reference for comparison or measurement (fiducial point).

item.title: Fiducial mark
item.description: index mark on a test system that allows automatic geometric identification and orientation detection of an object using imaging systems.

item.title: Field-of-regard
item.description: an area of the object space scanned by the field-of-view of a scanning sensor.

item.title: Field-of-view
item.description: the solid angle from which the detector receives radiation.

item.title: File Transfer Protocol
item.description: A protocol used to transfer files over an IP network.

item.title: Fixity Information
item.description: The information which documents the mechanisms that ensure that the Content Information object has not been altered in an undocumented manner.

item.title: Footprint
item.description: the area of a target encircled by the field-of-view of a detector of radiation, or irradiated by an active system.

item.title: Geodesy
item.description: The branch of applied mathematics that deals with the measurement of the shape and area of large tracts of country, the exact position of geographical points, and the curvature, shape, and dimensio...

item.title: Earth System Data Record
item.description: A unified and coherent set of observations of a given parameter of the Earth system, which is optimized to meet specific requirements in addressing science questions.

item.title: Engineering Data
item.description: Data which describe the physical condition and operation of the platform and instruments on the platform.

item.title: Event-based Order
item.description: An event is any environmental observation of significance.

item.title: Federated Archives
item.description: A group of Archives that has agreed to provide access to their holdings via one or more common finding aids.

item.title: Directory
item.description: “Discovery” service for what services are available in the PDGS, i.e.

item.title: Discipline
item.description: A field of study such as oceanography, meteorology, geology, or marine biology.

item.title: Discovery
item.description: Any service that helps the user to identify and locate EO resource starting from his needs.

item.title: Dissemination Information Package
item.description: [OAIS]Information Package - derived from one or more AIPs (Archival Information Package) -supplied to the Consumer in response to a request to the OAIS.

item.title: Documentation
item.description: The information component of a data set's associated knowledge may include mission, instrument, calibration, and other information in the form of text documents.

item.title: Downlink
item.description: Data stream from the satellite to a receiving station during visibility of the satellite from the station.

item.title: EO Space Data Holders and Archive Owners
item.description: This category is intended to include all entities dealing with the archiving of EO space data and responsible for their preservation in the long term.

item.title: Data set Series
item.description: Collection of data sets sharing the same product specification.

item.title: Detection limit
item.description: measured quantity value, obtained by a given measurement procedure, for which the probability of falsely claiming the absence of a component is β, given a probability α of falsely claiming its pres...

item.title: Digital Object Identifiers
item.description: Persistent identifiers that are globally unique character strings that reference physical, digital, or abstract objects.

item.title: Directory Service
item.description: Descriptions of metadata or data set catalogues accessed as a component of the Catalogue Service containing high level information suitable for making an initial determination of the potential usef...

item.title: Data System
item.description: (1) A collection of hardware and software to perform one or more specific data processing, analysis, storage, retrieval or distribution functions.

item.title: Data Time series
item.description: A data sequence that is designed to be consistent over a defined time interval.

item.title: Data Use Metrics
item.description: Data Use Metrics represent statistics on data access volumes, user groups, usefulness/popularity of tools and services.

item.title: Data User Guide
item.description: A document, either on-line or hardcopy, containing the necessary information for the correct usage of the data.

item.title: Data Lake
item.description: A data lake is a centralized repository that allows you to store all your structured and unstructured data at any scale.

item.title: Data Maturity
item.description: Beta

item.title: Data Mining
item.description: An analytical process that attempts to find correlations or patterns in large data sets for the purpose of data or knowledge discovery.

item.title: Data Model
item.description: An Earth science metadata model, which supports the data standardization necessary for total system interoperability within a heterogeneous, open systems environment.

item.title: Data Object
item.description: An entity that, together with associated Representation Information, is the original target of preservation.

item.title: Data Operator
item.description: A person who operates a computer in a datacenter and performs such activities as commanding the operating system, mounting disks and tapes and placing paper in the printer.

item.title: Data Colonialism
item.description: The process by which governments, non-governmental organizations and corporations claim ownership of and privatize the data that is produced by their users and citizens.

item.title: Data Democracy
item.description: Describes a methodological framework of values and actions that benefit and minimize any harm to the public or the typical user.

item.title: Data Dictionary
item.description: A formal repository of terms used to describe data.

item.title: Data File
item.description: A computer file that contains any type of data, including a word processing document or spreadsheet.

item.title: Data Governance
item.description: A set of processes that ensures that data assets are formally managed throughout the enterprise.

item.title: Data Acquisition
