item.description: Includes all stages of the process of acquiring a data product or services, beginning with the process for determining the need for the product or service and ending with the final stage of data st...

item.title: Data Architecture
item.description: Data architecture describes the structure of an organization's logical and physical data assets and data management resources.

item.title: Data Base
item.description: (1) A collection of data sets associated with a system, project, or facility.

item.title: Circulation
item.description: PDGS function implementing the distribution of products between facilities in the same or different geographic locations.

item.title: Citizen Data Scientist
item.description: A person who creates or generates models that leverage predictive or prescriptive analytics, but whose primary job function is outside of the field of statistics and analytics.

item.title: Cloud Optimized Formats
item.description: The format of data that is ideal in a cloud infrastructure to make them tractable to support cloud-native analysis or access.

item.title: Collection Group
item.description: Set of collections sharing a specific characteristic, e.

item.title: Common Data Elements
item.description: The purpose of the CDE Project is to standardize the collection of investigational data to facilitate comparison of results across studies and more effectively aggregate information into significan...

item.title: Common Services
item.description: The supporting services such as inter-process communication, name services, temporary storage allocation, exception handling, security, and directory services necessary to support the OAIS.

item.title: Compliance
item.description: A state of being in accordance with established guidelines or specifications.

item.title: Consolidation
item.description: Consolidation is the process to provide a set of products for long-term archiving and further processing.

item.title: Browse Image
item.description: Visual representation of a product (as an image) to help and support product selection in the frameof the user service facility.

item.title: Building Block
item.description: Set of functions with a well-defined external interface allowing reuse for different missions.

item.title: Bulk Data Transfer
item.description: A software-based mechanism that is designed to move large data files.

item.title: Bulk Dissemination
item.description: Bulk Dissemination is a special service provided in case of high volumes of data to be disseminated to User (Research on-demand or Operational).

item.title: Bulk Order
item.description: An order request for data from tape or disk cache which spans a month or more.

item.title: Certification Services
item.description: A NCEI director certification of archive authenticity.

item.title: Authenticity
item.description: The degree to which a person (or system) regards an object as what it is purported to be.

item.title: Authoritative Copy
item.description: Recognized as the official copy of the object from the originating source that has not been altered.

item.title: Availability
item.description: The quality of being at hand when needed.

item.title: Bias
item.description: - measurement bias: estimate of a systematic measurement error

item.title: Bias correction
item.description: Reconciling differences between sensors and/or products by a simple multiplication or addition of a correction factor.

item.title: Archive Organization
item.description: The organizational structure of an archive based on a sufficient number of qualified staffs with clear roles and responsibilities.

item.title: Archive Recommendation Package
item.description: An ARP is written in collaboration with the Data Producers early on in the process, in which recommendations are assembled for what exactly should be archived.

item.title: Artifact
item.description: - A physical object that is made or modified by human culture

item.title: Artificial Intelligence
item.description: The ability of a digital computer or computer-controlled robot to perform tasks commonly associated with intelligent beings.

item.title: Administrative Data
item.description: Data collected and maintained by agencies are used to administer (or run) programs and provide services to the public.

item.title: Aggregation
item.description: The presentation of many data files as one consistent block of time and space.

item.title: Analytics Optimized Data Store
item.description: Data stores offer reliable and flexible storage for processed data and make it available for queries and analysis on a large scale.

item.title: Accessibility
item.description: The capability to access available data.

item.title: Ad-hoc Order
item.description: A request that is generated by a Consumer for information the OAIS has indicated is currently available.

item.title: Access Collection
item.description: A collection of AIPs that is defined by a Collection Description but for which there is no Packaging Information for the collection in Archival Storage.
